root_text.an11.10:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati moranivāpe paribbājakārāme.
root_text.an11.10:2.1: “Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:2.3: Asekhena sīlakkhandhena, asekhena samādhikkhandhena, asekhena paññākkhandhena—
root_text.an11.10:2.4: imehi, kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:3.1: Aparehipi, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:3.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti, accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:4.1: Aparehipi, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:4.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:5.1: Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:5.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu accantaniṭṭho hoti accantayogakkhemī accantabrahmacārī accantapariyosāno seṭṭho devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an11.10:5.5: Brahmunā pesā, bhikkhave, sanaṅkumārena gāthā bhāsitā:
root_text.an11.10:7.1: Sā kho panesā, bhikkhave, sanaṅkumārena gāthā bhāsitā subhāsitā, no dubbhāsitā; atthasaṁhitā, no anatthasaṁhitā; anumatā mayā.

translation_text.an11.10:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, at the monastery of the wanderers in the peacocks’ feeding ground.
translation_text.an11.10:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an11.10:2.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant who has three qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:2.3: The entire spectrum of an adept’s ethics, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.an11.10:2.4: A mendicant with these three qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:3.1: A mendicant who has another three qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:3.3: A demonstration of psychic power, a demonstration of revealing, and a demonstration of instruction.
translation_text.an11.10:3.4: A mendicant with these three qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:4.1: A mendicant who has another three qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:4.3: Right view, right knowledge, and right freedom.
translation_text.an11.10:4.4: A mendicant with these three qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:5.1: A mendicant who has two qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:5.4: A mendicant with these two qualities has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate sanctuary from the yoke, the ultimate spiritual life, the ultimate goal. They are best among gods and humans.
translation_text.an11.10:5.5: The divinity Sanaṅkumāra also spoke this verse:
translation_text.an11.10:6.2: who take clan as the standard.
translation_text.an11.10:6.3: But one accomplished in knowledge and conduct
translation_text.an11.10:6.4: is first among gods and humans.’
translation_text.an11.10:7.1: Now, that verse spoken by the divinity Sanaṅkumāra is well spoken, not poorly spoken. It’s beneficial, not pointless, and I agree with it.
translation_text.an11.10:8.2: who take clan as the standard.
translation_text.an11.10:8.3: But one accomplished in knowledge and conduct
translation_text.an11.10:8.4: is first among gods and humans.’”
